title: Add a race-lap query-ranking specialist for pitting order within each field state
summary: Game-theoretic pit-stop work emphasizes that optimal timing depends on rivals, and CatBoost supports query/group ranking objectives directly.
rationale: Game-theoretic pit-stop work emphasizes that optimal timing depends on rivals, and CatBoost supports query/group ranking objectives directly. Instead of only pointwise `PitNextLap`, model each `(Year,Race,LapNumber)` as a query and learn who is most likely to pit next among the cars on track.
implementation_hint: Train a CatBoost `YetiRankPairwise` or `QueryCrossEntropy` specialist with query groups at `Year_Race_LapNumber`, using current numeric features plus field-relative ranks such as tyre-age percentile, lap-delta percentile, and position-pressure percentile within the same query. Blend its OOF score with the existing pointwise model rather than replacing it. Use `LeaveOneGroupOut` on `Year_Race` for all OOF generation.
expected_effect: Should sharpen relative ordering on crowded pit windows where several drivers are plausible stoppers and ROC AUC is driven by ranking quality more than absolute calibration.
risk: Query groups can be small or noisy, and ranking objectives can underperform if the engineered within-query context is weak. It is also easy to leak if query-normalized features are computed outside each fold.
